question: The scatter plot shows daily screen time vs. hours of sleep. Which statement is best supported by the data?

options.A: There is almost no relationship between screen time and sleep.
options.B: Sleep tends to increase as screen time increases.
options.C: People using the most screen time have by far the lowest sleep, as highlighted by the smallest, lightest points.
options.D: There is a generally negative relationship between screen time and sleep.

explanation: This scatter plot uses dual encoding: point position shows the relationship, but point size and color also encode the y-variable, making high values overly salient. That can tempt viewers to overstate the strength or form of the relationship.
